items.headline: Tuareg rebels seize Kidal as Russian forces withdraw from northern Mali
items.body: Tuareg and Al-Qaeda-linked militants forced Russia's Africa Corps to retreat from Kidal in Mali's largest offensive since 2012. The junta's defense minister was killed and President Goïta is reportedly in hiding after attacks on Bamako facilities.

items.headline: Pakistan military kills 216 militants in Balochistan after coordinated attacks
items.body: Security forces launched counter-operations across Balochistan following January 29 attacks by separatist militants on schools and military sites. The clashes killed 216 militants, 22 soldiers, and 36 civilians in one of the largest operations to date.

items.headline: Myanmar moves Aung San Suu Kyi to house arrest after five years detention
items.body: Military leader Min Aung Hlaing commuted the remaining sentence of the Nobel laureate to house arrest. State media released the first public photograph of Suu Kyi in years showing her flanked by uniformed personnel.

items.headline: Chad closes Sudan border after drone kills seventeen at funeral in Tine
items.body: A drone attack Wednesday killed mourners and children in the border town. President Déby blamed Sudan's RSF paramilitary and warned Chad would retaliate for any further strikes on its territory.

es.headline: Spanish Prime Minister accused of heading criminal organization in COVID mask corruption trial testimony
es.body: Businessman testified April 29 that Pedro Sánchez tops hierarchy of bribery scheme involving transport ministry contracts. Coalition partner Sumar and regional allies now threaten legislative support amid escalating government crisis.
